page.title: Blog
page.description: Recent writing on eBPF, userspace runtimes, AI agents, GPU systems, and systems research.

cards.title: An Empirical Study: AI Agent Rules Need Context and Layered Enforcement
cards.description: AI agent rules look simple in CLAUDE.md, but ActPlane's 2,116-statement study shows why context and layered OS enforcement decide what can be checked.

cards.title: Can an AI Agent Tune the Linux Scheduler? Inside SchedCP
cards.description: Linux scheduler tuning turns workload intent into measured tradeoffs, and SchedCP gives AI agents a verified schedext loop with 1.79x gains at 13x lower cost.

cards.title: Where AI Agent Budgets Go: Semantic Flamegraphs for Agent Traces
cards.description: AI agent traces hide budget hotspots inside thousands of prompts, and agentpprof uses semantic flamegraphs to group intent, tokens, time, files, and network.

cards.title: ActPlane: Pushing Agent Harness Enforcement Down to Kernel eBPF
cards.description: ActPlane is an eBPF-based policy engine that observes and enforces AI agent behavior at the OS kernel level. This post analyzes the systemic blind spots of prompt constraints, tool-layer guards, and sandboxes, and explains how ActPlane uses label propagation and temporal predicates to implement a deterministic agent harness.

cards.title: Runtime Observability and Enforcement for Opaque AI Agents with eBPF: Beyond Sandboxes and Approvals
cards.description: As AI coding agents run autonomously for hours inside harnesses and sandboxes the platform team may not own, approval-based control breaks down. This post argues for separating agent security into three layers (intent authorization, execution isolation, side-effect verification) and using eBPF-based observability (AgentSight) and enforcement (ActPlane) as an independent runtime observability and enforcement below the harness.

cards.title: When CPU Noise Slows Down GPU Inference: Measuring Scheduler and IRQ Impact with eBPF
cards.description: Quantitative eBPF tracing of CUDA kernel launches, scheduler context switches, and IRQs shows when CPU noise matters for GPU LLM inference and how CPU pinning recovers throughput.

cards.title: ACRFence: Preventing Semantic Rollback Attacks in Agent Checkpoint-Restore
cards.description: ACRFence explains semantic rollback attacks in AI agent checkpoint/restore workflows and shows how intent-aware fencing prevents duplicate irreversible actions and revived authority.

cards.title: AgentCgroup: What Happens When AI Coding Agents Meet OS Resources?
cards.description: AgentCgroup characterizes resource bursts in AI coding agents and uses eBPF, schedext, and cgroup v2 to enforce tool-call-granularity CPU and memory control without discarding agent state.

cards.title: Reverse Engineering Claude Code's SSL Traffic with eBPF
cards.description: Ever wondered what your AI coding agent is actually sending over the network? As part of our work on AgentSight — an eBPF-powered observability tool that monitors AI agents at system boundaries without modifying their

cards.title: A Taxonomy of GPU Bugs: 19 Defect Classes for CUDA Verification
cards.description: GPU programming introduces a distinct class of correctness and performance challenges that differ fundamentally from traditional CPU-based systems.

cards.title: Architectures for Agent Systems: A Survey of Isolation, Integration, and Governance
cards.description: Large Language Model (LLM) based agent systems – software that leverages LLMs to autonomously plan and execute multi-step tasks using external tools – are rapidly moving from proof-of-concept demos into enterprise

cards.title: The GPU Observability Gap: Why We Need eBPF on GPU devices
cards.description: As a revolutionary technology that provides programmability in the kernel, eBPF has achieved tremendous success in CPU observability, networking, and security.

cards.title: NVIDIA Open GPU Kernel Modules Comprehensive Source Code Analysis
cards.description: NVIDIA open GPU kernel modules expose 935K lines of driver architecture spanning nine GPU generations, from memory virtualization to NVLink interconnects.

cards.title: Understanding iaprof: A Deep Dive into AI/GPU Flame Graph Profiling
cards.description: Intel iaprof uses eBPF kernel tracing and GPU hardware sampling to build AI Flame Graphs that map CPU call stacks to specific GPU shader instruction stalls.

cards.title: AgentSight: Keeping Your AI Agents Under Control with eBPF-Powered System Observability
cards.description: Picture this: your AI agent is autonomously writing code and executing commands, but you have no idea what it's actually doing.

cards.title: Profiling and Tracing Tools Across System Layers and Architectures
cards.description: Profiling and tracing are complementary techniques for analyzing software performance and behavior.

cards.title: The Modern Memory Testing Arsenal -- A Complete Guide to Benchmarking Tools for Next-Gen Memory Systems
cards.description: Memory systems are evolving rapidly. From traditional DDR DRAM to high-bandwidth memory (HBM), persistent memory (PMEM), and the emerging Compute Express Link (CXL) technology, today's systems feature complex

cards.title: Observability, Profiling, and Debugging in Systems Conference (2015–2025)
cards.description: This survey reviews over a decade (2015–2025) of research on observability, profiling, and debugging techniques in computer systems, focusing on main-track papers from OSDI, SOSP, and EuroSys.

cards.title: Checkpoint/Restore Systems: Evolution, Techniques, and Applications in AI Agents
cards.description: Checkpoint/restore (C/R) technology – the ability to save a running program’s state to persistent storage and later resume execution from that point – has long been a cornerstone of fault tolerance and process

cards.title: ASPLOS 2025: Paper Summaries and Insights
cards.description: The Association for Computing Machinery's Architectural Support for Programming Languages and Operating Systems (ASPLOS) conference is a premier venue where researchers present cutting-edge work spanning computer

cards.title: EuroSys 2025 Paper Summaries and Analysis
cards.description: This post offers a detailed examination of papers accepted at EuroSys 2025, one of the premier conferences in computer systems research.

cards.title: GPU Profiling Under the Hood: An Implementation-Focused Survey of Modern Accelerator Tracing Tools
cards.description: Profiling and tracing heterogeneous accelerators (GPUs, DPUs, and APUs) is crucial for optimizing performance in modern systems.

cards.title: The Accelerator Toolkit: A Review of Profiling and Tracing for GPUs and other co-processor
cards.description: Modern computing increasingly relies on specialized accelerators – notably GPUs, DPUs, and APUs – to handle diverse workloads.

cards.title: OS-Level Challenges in LLM Inference and Optimizations
cards.description: Large Language Model (LLM) inference pushes computing systems to their limits, not only in raw compute but also in how the operating system (OS) manages resources.

cards.title: WASI and the WebAssembly Component Model: Current Status
cards.description: WebAssembly (WASM) has evolved from a browser-based technology into a promising runtime for server and embedded applications.

cards.title: eBPF Ecosystem Progress in 2024–2025: A Technical Deep Dive
cards.description: Extended Berkeley Packet Filter (eBPF) continues to rapidly evolve, cementing its role as a cornerstone for operating system extensibility.

cards.title: Security Vulnerabilities Study in Software Extensions and Plugins
cards.description: Software extensions and plugins allow customization and added features across many systems – from web servers and databases to browsers, IDEs, and CMS platforms.

cards.title: Can LLMs understand Linux kernel? A New AI-Powered Approach to Understanding Large Codebases
cards.description: Ever tried diving into a massive codebase like the Linux kernel and felt like you were swimming in an ocean of code with no land in sight?

cards.title: Building High-Performance Userspace eBPF VMs with LLVM
cards.description: We are excited to introduce llvmbpf, a new project aimed at empowering developers with a high-performance, multi-architecture eBPF virtual machine (VM) that leverages the LLVM framework for Just-In-Time (JIT) and

cards.title: The Past, Present, and Future of eBPF and Its Path to Revolutionizing Systems
cards.description: This blog post mainly references Alexei Starovoitov's presentation "Modernize BPF for the Next 10 Years" at BPFConf 2024.

cards.title: Simplifying Kernel Programming: The LLM-Powered eBPF Tool
cards.description: Kernel programming can be intimidating, requiring deep knowledge of operating system internals and programming constraints.

cards.title: The eBPF Evolution and Future: From Linux Origins to Cross-Platform Dominance
cards.description: eBPF (Extended Berkeley Packet Filter) has become a revolutionary force in operating system kernel programming since its inception.

cards.title: Implementing an Inline Hook in C in 5 minutes
cards.description: One of the fascinating aspects of programming comes when we try to alter the behavior of a program while it is running.

cards.title: The Evolution and Impact of eBPF: A list of Key Research Papers from Recent Years
cards.description: This is a list of eBPF related papers I read in recent years, might be helpful for people who are interested in eBPF related research.

cards.title: Introducing eunomia-bpf v1.0: Simplifying eBPF with CO-RE and WebAssembly
cards.description: The world of eBPF (Extended Berkeley Packet Filter) has been rapidly evolving, offering developers powerful tools to monitor and modify the behavior of systems at the kernel level.

cards.title: The Secure Path Forward for eBPF runtime: Challenges and Innovations
cards.description: Extended Berkeley Packet Filter (eBPF) represents a significant evolution in the way we interact with and extend the capabilities of modern operating systems.

cards.title: Userspace eBPF Runtimes: Overview and Applications
cards.description: In this blog post, we'll dive into the world of eBPF in userspace. While many are familiar with kernel-based eBPF, userspace eBPF runtimes have been making significant strides and offer compelling use cases. We will

cards.title: bpftime: Extending eBPF from Kernel to User Space
cards.description: eBPF is a revolutionary technology that originated in the Linux kernel, enabling sandboxed programs to run within the operating system's kernel.

cards.title: Use ChatGPT to write eBPF programs and trace the Linux kernel with natural language
cards.description: eBPF is a revolutionary technology that originated in the Linux kernel and allows sandboxed programs to run in the kernel of an operating system.

cards.title: Simplifying eBPF Development: GitHub Templates and Codespaces for Online Compilation and Execution
cards.description: Embarking on the eBPF journey can feel daunting, especially when confronted with setting up the perfect environment or making the ideal language choice.

cards.title: When Wasm Meets eBPF: Writing, Distributing, Loading, and Running eBPF Programs with WebAssembly
cards.description: In today's cloud-native world, eBPF and WebAssembly are two of the hottest lightweight code execution sandboxes/virtual machines.

cards.title: eBPF Advanced: Overview of New Kernel Features
cards.description: The Linux kernel primarily released versions 5.16-5.19, 6.0, and 6.1 in 2022, each of which introduced numerous new features for eBPF.

cards.title: Progress of eunomia-bpf in March
cards.description: The eunomia-bpf project is an open-source project aimed at providing a set of tools for writing and running eBPF programs more conveniently in the Linux kernel.

cards.title: eunomia-bpf 0.3.0 Release: Easily Build, Package, and Publish Full eBPF Applications by Writing Kernel-Mode Code
cards.description: eBPF, derived from BPF, is an efficient and flexible virtual machine component within the kernel.

cards.title: Wasm-bpf: Bridging WebAssembly and eBPF for Kernel Programmability
cards.description: Wasm was initially developed as a secure sandbox for browsers, and has since evolved into a high-performance, cross-platform, and multi-language software sandbox environment for cloud-native software components.

cards.title: Wasm-bpf: A Common eBPF Kernel Programmability for Cloud-Native Webassembly
cards.description: Originally developed with a browser-safe sandbox in mind, Wasm has evolved to make WebAssembly a high-performance, cross-platform and multilingual software sandbox environment for cloud-native software components, and

cards.title: eunomia-bpf: Looking forward to 2023, let eBPF sprout wings with Wasm
cards.description: Looking back at 2022, two technologies have received a lot of attention: eBPF and WebAssembly.

cards.title: 如何在 Linux 显微镜（LMP）项目中开启 eBPF 之旅？
cards.description: eBPF 为 Linux 内核提供了可扩展性，使开发人员能够对 Linux 内核进行编程，以便根据他们的业务需求快速构建智能的或丰富的功能。
cards.href: /blog/2022/10/11/如何在-linux-显微镜-lmp-项目中开启-ebpf-之旅/

cards.title: Running the ecli on Android 13
cards.description: This article mainly records the author's exploration process, results, and encountered issues when testing the support level of the high version Android Kernel for CO-RE technology based on libbpf in the Android Studio

cards.title: 在 WebAssembly 中编写 eBPF 程序和使用 libbpf
cards.description: eBPF (extended Berkeley Packet Filter) is a high-performance kernel virtual machine that runs in the kernel space and is used to collect system and network information.

cards.title: 在 WebAssembly 中使用 Rust 编写 eBPF 程序并发布 OCI 镜像
cards.description: eBPF（extended Berkeley Packet Filter）是一种高性能的内核虚拟机，可以运行在内核空间中，以收集系统和网络信息。随着计算机技术的不断发展，eBPF 的功能日益强大，并且已经成为各种效率高效的在线诊断和跟踪系统，以及构建安全的网络、服务网格的重要组成部分。
cards.href: /blog/2022/02/11/在-webassembly-中使用-rust-编写-ebpf-程序并发布-oci-镜像/

description.en: Recent writing on eBPF, userspace runtimes, AI agents, GPU systems, and systems research.
description.zh: 关于 eBPF、userspace runtime、AI agent、GPU systems 和系统研究的近期文章。

blogEntries.title: An Empirical Study: AI Agent Rules Need Context and Layered Enforcement
blogEntries.description: AI agent rules look simple in CLAUDE.md, but ActPlane's 2,116-statement study shows why context and layered OS enforcement decide what can be checked.
blogEntries.excerpt: A rule like "run the full test suite before committing" looks simple until an AI coding agent edits a source file after the last test run and then calls git commit.

blogEntries.title: Can an AI Agent Tune the Linux Scheduler? Inside SchedCP
blogEntries.description: Linux scheduler tuning turns workload intent into measured tradeoffs, and SchedCP gives AI agents a verified schedext loop with 1.79x gains at 13x lower cost.
blogEntries.excerpt: A scheduler that makes a Linux kernel build faster can make schbench latency worse.

blogEntries.title: Where AI Agent Budgets Go: Semantic Flamegraphs for Agent Traces
blogEntries.description: AI agent traces hide budget hotspots inside thousands of prompts, and agentpprof uses semantic flamegraphs to group intent, tokens, time, files, and network.
blogEntries.excerpt: A $3000 AI-agent bill is not a diagnosis. It does not tell you whether the money went to code review, debugging, documentation, tool retries, or continuation prompts that stretched one task across many turns. The

blogEntries.title: ActPlane: Pushing Agent Harness Enforcement Down to Kernel eBPF
blogEntries.description: ActPlane is an eBPF-based policy engine that observes and enforces AI agent behavior at the OS kernel level. This post analyzes the systemic blind spots of prompt constraints, tool-layer guards, and sandboxes, and explains how ActPlane uses label propagation and temporal predicates to implement a deterministic agent harness.
blogEntries.excerpt: You wrote a rule in CLAUDE.md: "do not run git push." The agent obeyed, and it never called the git tool.

blogEntries.title: Runtime Observability and Enforcement for Opaque AI Agents with eBPF: Beyond Sandboxes and Approvals
blogEntries.description: As AI coding agents run autonomously for hours inside harnesses and sandboxes the platform team may not own, approval-based control breaks down. This post argues for separating agent security into three layers (intent authorization, execution isolation, side-effect verification) and using eBPF-based observability (AgentSight) and enforcement (ActPlane) as an independent runtime observability and enforcement below the harness.
blogEntries.excerpt: AI coding agents now run for hours, complete entire features end-to-end, optimize production GPU kernels, and merge thousands of pull requests autonomously.

blogEntries.title: When CPU Noise Slows Down GPU Inference: Measuring Scheduler and IRQ Impact with eBPF
blogEntries.description: Quantitative eBPF tracing of CUDA kernel launches, scheduler context switches, and IRQs shows when CPU noise matters for GPU LLM inference and how CPU pinning recovers throughput.
blogEntries.excerpt: GPU inference often looks like a GPU problem, but the CPU still sits on the critical path.

blogEntries.title: ACRFence: Preventing Semantic Rollback Attacks in Agent Checkpoint-Restore
blogEntries.description: ACRFence explains semantic rollback attacks in AI agent checkpoint/restore workflows and shows how intent-aware fencing prevents duplicate irreversible actions and revived authority.
blogEntries.excerpt: AI agent frameworks are bringing checkpoint/restore, time travel, and rewind into everyday developer workflows.

blogEntries.title: AgentCgroup: What Happens When AI Coding Agents Meet OS Resources?
blogEntries.description: AgentCgroup characterizes resource bursts in AI coding agents and uses eBPF, schedext, and cgroup v2 to enforce tool-call-granularity CPU and memory control without discarding agent state.
blogEntries.excerpt: An AI coding agent spends several quiet minutes reading and editing files, then launches pytest.

blogEntries.title: Reverse Engineering Claude Code's SSL Traffic with eBPF
blogEntries.description: Ever wondered what your AI coding agent is actually sending over the network? As part of our work on AgentSight — an eBPF-powered observability tool that monitors AI agents at system boundaries without modifying their

blogEntries.excerpt: In May 2022, NVIDIA made a decision that would fundamentally alter the landscape of GPU computing on Linux: they open-sourced the kernel-mode components of their GPU driver.

blogEntries.slug: 在-webassembly-中使用-rust-编写-ebpf-程序并发布-oci-镜像
blogEntries.title: 在 WebAssembly 中使用 Rust 编写 eBPF 程序并发布 OCI 镜像
blogEntries.description: eBPF（extended Berkeley Packet Filter）是一种高性能的内核虚拟机，可以运行在内核空间中，以收集系统和网络信息。随着计算机技术的不断发展，eBPF 的功能日益强大，并且已经成为各种效率高效的在线诊断和跟踪系统，以及构建安全的网络、服务网格的重要组成部分。
blogEntries.excerpt: eBPF（extended Berkeley Packet Filter）是一种高性能的内核虚拟机，可以运行在内核空间中，以收集系统和网络信息。随着计算机技术的不断发展，eBPF 的功能日益强大，并且已经成为各种效率高效的在线诊断和跟踪系统，以及构建安全的网络、服务网格的重要组成部分。
